title: chrysanthemum subglaucum
intro: Chrysanthemum subglaucum, also known as 'Zi Wan' or 'Imperial Herb', is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family. It is a traditional Chinese medicinal herb that has been used for centuries to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum subglaucum include reducing fever, alleviating headache and eye problems, and supporting immune function, making it a popular ingredient in traditional Chinese medicine. Chrysanthemum subglaucum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as tea, capsules, and tinctures, where it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
